senses.definition: a wall that has been set or placed as part of a structure; a wall that is set in place
examples: The builders raised a setwall along the boundary.
They lined the courtyard with a setwall for privacy.

senses.definition: plural of setwall; walls that have been placed or built as part of a structure
examples: The setwalls around the garden were repaired.
They inspected the setwalls for cracks.

senses.definition: having a set of rules, ideas, or preferences; oriented or inclined toward a particular set of beliefs or behaviors
examples: He is setwise about punctuality and never arrives late.
Their setwise approach to the project helped streamline decisions.

senses.definition: a group of related songs, movements, or tasks that are performed together as part of a show or exercise
examples: The dance troupe rehearsed the setwork for the performance.
The teacher assigned a setwork to practice for the recital.

senses.definition: plural of setwork; multiple sequences of songs or tasks grouped for performance or practice
examples: The company produced several setworks for the tour.
They reviewed the setworks before the rehearsal.

senses.definition: a proper noun or oblique term; may refer to a personal or place name in certain contexts
examples: In the historical record, Seudah appears as a name variant.
The map lists Seudah as a locality.

senses.definition: a trench or ditch in a landscape; sometimes used poetically to mean a low area or hollow
examples: The rain filled the seugh after the storm.
They walked along the seugh to reach the low-lying field.

senses.definition: an abbreviation or short form for seven or a related term in specific domains
examples: The code used sev to denote seven in the dataset.
They logged sev units of material.

senses.definition: a city in Crimea, historically important as a naval base; widely known from historical events
examples: Sevastopol played a key role in naval history.
The museum in Sevastopol houses many artifacts from the siege.

senses.definition: informal or historical variant spelling of seven, or a short form in specific dialects
examples: In some old texts, seve is used instead of seven.
They counted up to seve in the tradition.

senses.definition: the number 7; a quantity of seven things
examples: She has seven apples.
They met at seven o'clock.

senses.definition: consisting of seven parts or items; sevenfold
examples: Seven days make a week.
They reached the seven-stage plan.

senses.definition: a plant or tree species with seven-branching bark in some contexts; a compound common in grafting or horticulture
examples: The gardener trimmed the sevenbark carefully.
Sevenbark is known for its rugged bark texture.

senses.definition: a person or thing that comes seventh; in some contexts a religious position or a person who observes seven liturgical hours
examples: A sevener in the council was appointed.
The sevener watched the liturgy with reverence.

senses.definition: consisting of seven parts or times as great as seven
examples: The plan increases sevenfold in scope.
Their influence grew sevenfold over the decade.

senses.definition: to seven times as much or as many; by seven times
examples: Prices rose sevenfold in a few years.
The output improved sevenfold after the upgrade.

senses.definition: having been folded seven times or multiplied sevenfold; used poetically or descriptively
examples: The sevenfolded cloth shimmered in the light.
A sevenfolded pattern ran along the edge.

senses.definition: the state of being sevenfold; the quality of having sevenfoldness
examples: The sevenfoldness of the tale adds a rich complexity.
She admired the sevenfoldness in the design.

senses.definition: a period of seven nights; an old or poetic term for a week
examples: They waited sevennight before the decision.
Traditions required a sevennight vigil.

senses.definition: seven pence; a coin amount of sevenpence in old currency systems
examples: The item cost sevenpence in the old market.
He paid sevenpence and received a small bookmark.

senses.definition: plural of sevenpence; multiple sevenpence coins
examples: She found sevenpences in the drawer.
The purse contained several sevenpences.

senses.definition: alternative plural for sevenpence; informal spelling variant
examples: He asked for sevenpennies as change.
The old shop still prices items in sevenpennies.

senses.definition: made of seven pence or costing sevenpence; historical coinage-related term
examples: The sevenpenny stamp was common in the era.
A sevenpenny item is inexpensive.

senses.definition: the plural of seven; a number of seven things
examples: She bought sevens of apples.
They scored sevens in the game.

senses.definition: the number 700; seven hundred
examples: The building cost seven score dollars, roughly 700.
An old ledger lists seven score units.

senses.definition: the number 17; a quantity of seventeen things
examples: She is seventeen years old.
There are seventeen chapters in the book.

senses.definition: seven times seven; multiplied by seventeen in some contexts
examples: The growth was seventeenfold over the decade.
They saw a seventeenfold increase in sales.
